cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at this question and the passage. The question is asking what can be concluded from Isabelle Dinoire's remarks. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I'll go through the passage again to find the relevant parts. The passage mentions that Isabelle had a severe dog attack, which destroyed her nose, lips, and chin. She was the first person to have a face transplant. After the operation, she saw her face in the mirror and said, "I was scared to look at myself, but when I did it was already marvelous and I couldn't believe it. I thought it would be blue and swollen but it was already beautiful." Now, looking at the options: A) She was too shocked to see herself in the mirror. But the passage says she was scared, but when she looked, it was "marvelous" and "beautiful." So she did look and wasn't too shocked to the point of not seeing it. So A might not be correct. B) She was satisfied with the result of the operation. Her comments about it being "marvelous" and "beautiful" indicate satisfaction. Also, later she says "I have been saved" and people said she should go on, which implies she's happy with the outcome. So B seems a good candidate. C) She thought her damaged face couldn't be repaired. The passage says doctors offered her little hope using conventional surgery, but she underwent a transplant. Her own remarks don't say she thought it couldn't be repaired, rather the doctors did. So C is probably not correct. D)
